tagline: The honest voice in a shill-filled space.

budget.note: Hard cap. The LLM client aborts the run (fail-closed) before exceeding either ceiling. Costed from response usage; see llm.py PRICING. Sized for claude-fable-5 on the judgment stages (its always-on thinking bills as output tokens); a typical run lands well under $1.50.

models.note: Quality-first lineup (owner's explicit call, 2026-07-10): claude-fable-5 (Anthropic's most capable model) on the two JUDGMENT stages, editor (rank + de-shill) and verifier (adversarial source audit); claude-opus-4-8 on the writer (strong news prose, human-reviewed anyway, and a different model family after two Fable stages). Fable's thinking is always on and counts against max_tokens, hence the larger caps. The verifier remains a SEPARATE call with a distinct, adversarial prompt so it does not rubber-stamp the editor. No temperature/top_p/top_k and no thinking param is sent: sampling params are rejected (HTTP 400) on this model family, and omitting thinking runs Fable's adaptive thinking. llm.py adds a server-side refusal fallback to claude-opus-4-8 on Fable calls because hack/exploit coverage can trip its cyber safety classifiers. claude-opus-4-8 everywhere is the cheaper fallback lineup; claude-sonnet-5 the cheapest.

sources.note: source_tier drives editorial weight. primary = official/primary sources (trust most). major = established outlets. aggregator = purpose-built crypto aggregator (many sources, use its own signals). Feeds are free and keyless. CryptoPanic and X are optional and key-gated (see below); absence is documented, never a failure.

cryptopanic.note: Best single starting aggregator (hundreds of sources + vote/sentiment signals). Set CRYPTOPANIC_TOKEN to enable. Skipped, not failed, when the token is absent.

x_twitter.query: (crypto OR bitcoin OR ethereum OR SEC) -is:retweet -is:reply lang:en
x_twitter.note: News breaks here FIRST, but X's API is paid (about $100/mo for the basic tier). Wired against X API v2 recent search; set X_BEARER_TOKEN to enable. Skipped, not failed, when absent. Edit 'query' to follow specific accounts (from:handle) or cashtags. Could not be live-tested here (no token).

whale_alert.note: Large on-chain moves from Whale Alert's FREE public alert archive (keyless; their keyed v1 REST API was retired behind $29.95/mo WebSocket and $699/mo Enterprise plans, neither of which fits -- see DEVIATIONS D7). The archive is newest-first and refreshed continuously; the loader in common.py streams just the window's slice. Only very large moves ($50M+ roughly) appear, which suits the brief. A fetch failure is a documented skip, never a pipeline failure. Capped at max_items so on-chain data cannot flood the brief. Feeds the Whale Watch board too (whale_flows.py).

whale_flows.note: The follow-the-money board (whale_flows.py). Classifies large transfers as exchange inflow (onto exchanges; potential sell pressure) vs outflow (off exchanges; accumulation) and aggregates net flow per asset. Uses the same Whale Alert source/key as above. Market data, not news.

dedupe.note: Near-identical stories across outlets (same event, many rewrites) collapse into one cluster by normalized-headline token overlap or shared URL. The cluster keeps the highest-tier source as primary and lists the rest as corroboration.

publish.not_financial_advice: Crypto Cronkite reports events. It never advises trades. Nothing here is financial advice.

newsletter.note: Owned audience, highest value, build first. Set endpoint + key to enable.

video.note: Newscast script handoff to the recording workflow.
